summary: Closed, apparently unmerged menu cleanup PR touching 31 files. The slice shows broad renaming and type-clarification work around menu/main-rule code, minor-scene load/leave data, controller input bitmasks/repeat handling, and game-mode structs. It added menu forward declarations and new MenuEnterData/MenuExitData structs, renamed several anonymous or offset-based fields to behavior-derived names, and introduced mnruleplus source/header work. Evidence for matching results is limited to the body text saying "mnmain work & matches" and "mnruleplus work" plus the large mnmain/mnruleplus diffs; no review comments are present.
what_changed: Added many Dolphin PAD bitmask aliases beyond the basic button constants, including stick/substick direction bits, PAD_TRIGGER_LR, PAD_CONFIRM, PAD_CANCEL, PAD_LRAST, and PAD_ANY_* direction masks, using 64-bit constants where masks exceed 32 bits.
Added gm forward declarations for MenuExitData and MenuEnterData.
Changed fn_8017280C declaration in gm_16F1.h from unknown return/params to void fn_8017280C(void).

Renamed MinorSceneInfo callback payload fields from unk_struct_0 and unk_struct_1 to load_data and leave_data in gm/types.h and propagated the names through gm_1A3F.c, gm_1A45.c, gm_1B03.c, gm_1BA8.c, and other call sites.
Added comments to gm_801A4B90 and gm_801A4B9C identifying them as returning current scene enter/exit data.
Added explicit MenuEnterData and MenuExitData structs in gm/types.h with STATIC_ASSERT size checks of 0x4, documenting MJ_MENU OnLoad/OnLeave payloads.

Renamed TmData fields from raw offsets to menu-rule semantics: cur_option, match_type, combatants, entrants, hmn_cpu_count, stage_selection_type, and cpu_level, with uses updated in tournament/menu code.

Renamed controller-map fields in gm_1A36.static.h and gm_1A36.c: unk became repeat2 and x28 became repeat_timer; trigger received an inline comment as buttons pressed this frame with uncertainty noted.
Renamed gmm_x1868 field unk_4 to x186C and documented it as an unlocked-features bitmask, while preserving uncertainty about adjacent unused padding.
Added or moved substantial menu work into mnmain.c and mnruleplus.c, with mnruleplus.c and mnruleplus.h newly populated in this slice.
Adjusted one MinorSceneHandler entry in gmscdata.c by casting mn_8022DDA8_OnEnter to void*, likely to satisfy the handler signature after type cleanup.
Touched soundtest interface files, though the excerpt provides only partial evidence for those changes.
smart_moves: Propagated semantic field names across all visible users instead of only changing struct declarations, reducing future confusion around minor-scene callback data and menu state.
Kept uncertain discoveries explicitly marked with comments such as "maybe rename?" and remarks about unused padding, which avoids overstating decomp certainty.
Used STATIC_ASSERT on the new MenuEnterData and MenuExitData structs to lock the ABI/layout while introducing better names.
Separated load_data and leave_data terminology according to actual OnLoad and OnLeave callback use, making scene-transition code easier to audit.
Introduced 64-bit PAD masks with 1ULL for synthesized or high-order menu-input masks, avoiding truncation for values beyond bit 31.
Renamed TmData fields based on repeated usage contexts such as selected option, entrants, match type, and stage selection type, rather than leaving raw x0/x4/x14 offsets in menu logic.
Documented that x186C behaves like an unlocked-features bitmask while preserving offset-style naming, a cautious midpoint when exact feature names are not fully known.
decomp_lessons: For menu and game-mode decomp, MinorSceneInfo field names should reflect callback direction: load_data is passed to OnLoad and leave_data is passed to OnLeave.
When adding semantic structs for callback payloads, include size assertions immediately; these small scene structs are layout-sensitive even when only four bytes.
Input helper structs may contain multiple related masks: button, trigger, repeat, release, and repeat-like values should be named from update behavior and aggregation logic.
If a bitmask grows past 32 bits, define constants with 1ULL so decompiled comparisons and OR operations preserve the intended type width.
Offset-style names such as x186C may be preferable to a guessed final semantic name when only the category of the field is understood.
Large menu cleanup PRs can mix matching work, struct naming, and header surgery; future work should split or document these changes carefully for reviewability.
Casts to void* in scene-handler tables may be necessary when callback prototypes remain broader than the concrete menu function signatures.
naming_conventions: struct MenuEnterData for data passed to MJ_MENU OnLoad
struct MenuExitData for data passed to MJ_MENU OnLeave
TmData::x18-equivalent field added as cpu_level with padding adjusted to _x1C
gmm_x1868::unk_4 -> x186C, documented as unlocked features bitmask
PAD constants use uppercase Dolphin-style names, including PAD_STICK_*, PAD_SUBSTICK_*, PAD_ANY_*, PAD_CONFIRM, and PAD_CANCEL
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Changed fn_8017280C to an exact void(void) prototype, which can improve call-site typing and reduce reliance on UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS.
Maintained struct offsets in comments while renaming fields, helping preserve layout reasoning for matching.
Used STATIC_ASSERT(sizeof(struct MenuEnterData) == 0x4) and STATIC_ASSERT(sizeof(struct MenuExitData) == 0x4) to guard against accidental layout drift.
Adjusted handler-table typing with an explicit (void*) cast for mn_8022DDA8_OnEnter, suggesting a practical compromise when function pointer types are not yet fully reconciled.
The PR body claims mnmain matches and mnruleplus work, but the excerpt does not provide enough detail to identify specific nonmatching-to-matching tactics in those files.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR context.
The PR was closed with merged_at null, so any conclusions should be treated as evidence from an unmerged cleanup branch rather than accepted project guidance.
follow_up_queries: Check whether MenuEnterData/MenuExitData and MinorSceneInfo load_data/leave_data names were later accepted in another PR.
Inspect the full mnmain.c and mnruleplus.c diffs to identify exactly which functions matched and whether any local patterns should be reused.
Verify whether gm_controller_map::repeat2 is still the best name or whether later work established a clearer semantic name.
Audit PAD_CONFIRM/PAD_CANCEL/PAD_ANY_* usage to confirm the intended synthesized menu-input mask semantics.
Look for later review or replacement PRs after this unmerged branch was closed.
